summary: Matched the Motion-Sensor Bomb common item implementation by replacing itmsbomb.c placeholder declarations with concrete C functions, adding the MsBomb item state table symbol, typing the MsBomb item variable bitfield, and moving itmsbomb.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The PR also propagated the newly named ItemStateTable_MsBomb symbol through item logic table references and adjusted GALE01 split ranges after data/sdata/sdata2 size changes.
what_changed: Implemented the previously stubbed itmsbomb.c functions for Motion-Sensor Bomb behavior, including spawn, pickup, drop, throw, collision, physics, damage, reflection, shield, and explosion-related handlers.
Added a local itMsBomb_Attrs struct in itmsbomb.c containing two floats and an itECB, with STATIC_ASSERT(sizeof(itMsBomb_Attrs) == 24).
Defined ItemStateTable_MsBomb[] with eight item states and wired it to itMsbomb_UnkMotion* callbacks.
Renamed the data symbol at 0x803F5E00 from the anonymous it_803F5E00 to ItemStateTable_MsBomb in symbols.txt and code references.
Updated it_2725.c and the commented table in it_3F14.c so the MSBomb logic table references ItemStateTable_MsBomb.
Added itMsBomb_ItemVars to itCommonItems.h as a one-byte bitfield struct xDD4_b0 through xDD4_b7, with a size static assert.
Added the msbomb member to the Item union field xDD4_itemVar in src/melee/it/types.h.
Replaced UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS and anonymous Item_GObj* prototypes in itmsbomb.h with typed signatures for the matched functions.
Changed configure.py so melee/it/items/itmsbomb.c is built as Matching instead of NonMatching.
Adjusted config/GALE01/splits.txt section ends for itmsbomb.c data, sdata, and sdata2 from 0x803F5EA8/0x804D52C0/0x804DCAA8 to 0x803F5EA5/0x804D52BD/0x804DCAA4.
smart_moves: Introduced ItemStateTable_MsBomb as a descriptive global name and updated both live and commented logic-table references, making future item table work more searchable than the raw address symbol it_803F5E00.
Modeled the xDD4 item-specific storage as a named msbomb union member with explicit bitfields, allowing code to use ip->xDD4_itemVar.msbomb.xDD4_b0 instead of opaque raw access.

Used small static inline helpers for repeated sequences around it_80290314, damage received, and touched/explosion handling, likely to preserve matching codegen while avoiding duplicated source blocks.
Kept struct sizing explicit with STATIC_ASSERT for both the special attributes struct and the item variable bitfield, protecting layout-sensitive item code.
Updated build classification, symbols, and split metadata in the same PR as the code match, reducing drift between source, linker config, and decomp status.
decomp_lessons: For item matches, expect to add both behavior code and the item-specific xDD4 union type; matching often depends on giving the per-item itemVar storage the correct bitfield layout.
When a data table becomes understood, rename the symbols.txt entry and replace all references to the raw it_803Fxxxx name with a semantic ItemStateTable_* name.
Matched item state-table files should update configure.py from NonMatching to Matching only after the source, header prototypes, symbols, and splits are consistent.
Local special-attribute structs with static asserts are useful for reading xC4_article_data->x4_specialAttributes without over-generalizing evidence into global headers.
Inline helper functions and PAD_STACK calls are valid matching tactics for preserving stack shape and codegen in item behavior functions.
Header cleanup is part of matching: replacing UNK_RET and UNK_PARAMS with Item_GObj* and bool/void signatures makes downstream item logic easier to type.
naming_conventions: Use ItemStateTable_<ItemName> for named item state table globals, as shown by ItemStateTable_MsBomb.
Use it<ItemName>_ItemVars for per-item xDD4 item variable structs, as shown by itMsBomb_ItemVars.
Use a lowercase item member in the Item xDD4_itemVar union, as shown by msbomb.
Use it<ItemName>_Attrs for item-specific special attributes when only locally needed, as shown by itMsBomb_Attrs.
Preserved existing function naming for unknown item motions as itMsbomb_UnkMotionN_Anim/Phys/Coll and existing address-style functions like it_80290314 where semantics were not fully named.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK(8), PAD_STACK(0x10), and PAD_STACK(0x18) in several functions to maintain matching stack layout.

Used static inline helpers to force shared instruction sequences while preserving caller-specific stack/codegen, including it_80290314_inline, it_3F14_Logic19_DmgReceived_inline, and it_802908D8_inline.
Copied item ECB data through a local itECB variable in thrown-state setup before passing it to it_80275D5C, suggesting careful shaping of assignment/codegen.
Kept exact float literal values in lb_800119DC call, including 1.0f, 0.02f, and 1.0471976f.
Adjusted split end addresses after matching because emitted data/sdata/sdata2 sizes changed by a few bytes.
follow_up_queries: Find other common item PRs that introduce ItemStateTable_* names and xDD4_itemVar union members.
Search for remaining references to raw item state table symbols like it_803Fxxxx in config/GALE01/symbols.txt and item logic tables.
Compare itmsbomb.c inline helper and PAD_STACK patterns with other matched item files for reusable matching tactics.
Check whether itMsBomb_Attrs should remain local or eventually move to a shared item attributes header if other code starts referencing it.
